meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Project Management to Solve Debugging Struggles
content.seo_description: Learn to master project management and debug like a pro. A beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your coding journey.
content.answer_block: Debugging is not a skill deficiency; it is a project management failure. By structuring your code into isolated modules and using AI-assisted tracing, you transform mysterious bugs into manageable tasks. Use Antigravity to map logic flow and Claude Code to implement systematic fixes, turning every error into a learning milestone.
content.intro:
The screen is a frozen landscape of red text, and your cursor pulses with the rhythmic mocking of an unresolved stack trace. You have spent hours chasing a ghost in the machine, feeling the familiar weight of imposter syndrome creeping in. You aren't just stuck; you are currently navigating the rite of passage every senior engineer endured before you.

But here is the secret: debugging is 20% syntax and 80% project management. When you treat your code like a professional architectural project rather than a messy scratchpad, the bugs lose their power. With the right orchestration—Antigravity for structural clarity and Claude Code for surgical execution—you will stop guessing and start engineering. Let's reclaim your momentum.

steps.title: Define the Scope of Failure
steps.description: Before fixing, isolate the bug. Document exactly what is failing, the expected output, and the actual output to prevent scope creep during the debugging session.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this codebase and create a dependency map for the module currently throwing errors. Identify all inputs and side effects associated with the failed function.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'summarize the last three stack traces and identify the common variable state across them'

steps.title: Implement Modular Logging
steps.description: Incorporate observability to make the invisible visible. By adding targeted logs, you create a trail of breadcrumbs that makes the logic flow undeniable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this function to include structured logging at every conditional branch, ensuring we can track the execution path without cluttering the console.

steps.title: Isolate Logic with Unit Tests
steps.description: A bug is just a test case you haven't written yet. Create a sandbox environment to test the failing logic in isolation, removing external dependencies.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a comprehensive unit test suite for this specific function, covering edge cases like null inputs and empty arrays.

steps.title: Execute Surgical Refactoring
steps.description: Once the failure point is identified, apply a surgical fix. Avoid 'shotgun debugging' by changing only one variable at a time.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Propose a refactor for the identified bottleneck that improves readability and handles the edge case identified in step 3.

steps.title: Verify and Document
steps.description: After the fix, verify the entire system. Document the solution in your README so you never have to solve this specific bug twice.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the recent changes and explain how they resolve the initial bug while maintaining overall system performance.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit --message 'Resolved logic error in user authentication module'

pro_tips: Treat your error messages as data, not insults; copy-paste them into a dedicated 'Bug Log' document to spot repeating patterns.
If you have been staring at a bug for 30 minutes, step away; your brain is likely suffering from 'code blindness' that AI cannot fix.
Always build your MVP with a 'debug-first' architecture, ensuring your core functions are decoupled from the UI from day one.
Use Claude Code to explain code you didn't write—it is the fastest way to build mental models of new libraries.
Maintain a 'Change Log' for every session; knowing exactly what you touched last is the ultimate defense against regression bugs.
content.next_steps: Once your first app is stable, focus on implementing CI/CD pipelines to automate your testing, ensuring that your future code stays bug-free by design.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are a developer who understands that debugging is a structured, conquerable process. Take these tools, apply the discipline, and watch your productivity soar. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
